page: Amazon Web Services inf2.xlarge is an instance within the inf2 family, utilizing the Nitro hypervisor. It is configured with an x86_64 AMD EPYC 7R13 processor running at 3.6 GHz, featuring 2 physical cores and 4 vCPUs with dedicated allocation. The system includes 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory operating at 3 GHz, yielding 4.0 GB of RAM per core. There is no local storage bundled with this instance, and baseline network bandwidth is rated at 2 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates a divergence between single-core and multi-core performance. The single-core CPU performance is strong, placing in the top 25% of stress-ng tests, and Geekbench single-core scores sit in the average tier. However, multi-core performance is weak, falling into the bottom 25% for both stress-ng and Geekbench multi-core evaluations. Memory bandwidth is also limited, with both small-block cached and large-block uncached read/write operations scoring in the bottom 25%.

For specialized tasks, the instance achieves average performance in single-threaded gzip compression and bzip3 decompression, but drops to weak performance under multi-threaded decompression. Database operations, Redis SET operations, and static web serving throughput are consistently positioned in the bottom 25%. LLM inference benchmarks on a small 135M model show average text generation speeds of 153.84 tokens per second, but prompt processing speeds and larger 7B model inference tasks perform poorly. This profile suggests the instance is suited for lightweight general-purpose workloads or single-threaded applications that benefit from dedicated CPU allocation, rather than memory-intensive or highly parallelized database and web serving tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services inf2.xlarge is a general-purpose instance powered by an AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 4 vCPUs, 2 physical cores, and 16.0 GB of DDR4 memory. Operating on the Nitro hypervisor, it features a 3.6 GHz clock speed and dedicated CPU allocation, but lacks local storage and is limited to a 2 Gbps baseline network bandwidth. Performance benchmarks reveal a notable contrast: single-core CPU execution is strong, ranking in the top 25%, whereas multi-core performance, memory bandwidth, and database operations fall into the bottom 25%. While the instance handles single-threaded compression and small-model text generation at average speeds, it struggles with multi-threaded workloads, large-model prompt processing, and high-throughput web serving. The inf2.xlarge is best suited for lightweight, single-threaded applications and general-purpose tasks that do not require high memory bandwidth or local storage.
og_description: Amazon Web Services inf2.xlarge is an x86_64 instance with 4 vCPUs, 16 GB RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC CPU, offering strong single-core performance but limited multi-core and memory bandwidth.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services inf2.xlarge is an x86_64 instance featuring 4 vCPUs, 16 GB DDR4 RAM, and a 3.6 GHz AMD EPYC CPU with dedicated allocation.
tagline: A dedicated x86_64 virtual instance delivering strong single-core processing capabilities balanced with standard memory capacity for non-storage-intensive workloads.
bullet_points: AMD EPYC 7R13 processor with 4 dedicated vCPUs and 3.6 GHz clock speed
16.0 GB of DDR4 system memory with no local storage
Strong single-core CPU performance contrasting with weak multi-core scaling
Low memory bandwidth and 2 Gbps baseline network speed
Suited for single-threaded tasks and lightweight general-purpose workloads
